serving.description: 1 whole crab (~500 g whole weight, approximately 200–250 g edible meat and sauce)
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical restaurant serving of chilli crab is one whole mud crab weighing approximately 500 grams, yielding roughly 200–250 grams of edible crab meat and sauce combined.

nutrient_source.title: Chilli Crab — HPB Singapore Foodo / Energy and Nutrient Composition of Food (Singapore)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: HPB Singapore Foodo lists Chilli Crab as a named dish entry. Per-100g values are taken directly from that entry. The dish is a canonical Singaporean seafood dish and HPB is the primary approved regional source.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; supplementary data for mixed seafood dishes and analogous protein-dominant cooked dishes
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Chilli Crab. GI is estimated at 35 based on analogous protein-dominant, low-carbohydrate cooked seafood dishes with tomato-based sauce from the University of Sydney GI Database. The dish is predominantly protein and fat with moderate sauce-derived carbohydrates (5.8 g/100g), supporting a low GI classification. GL per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (35 × 11.6) / 100 ≈ 4.1, using 200 g edible portion × 5.8 g carbohydrate/100g = 11.6 g carbohydrate per serving.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~420 mg/100g; ~840 mg per 200g edible serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit chilli crab consumption due to its high sodium content from soy sauce and chilli paste; a single serving can contribute approximately 35–40% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~35) and moderate carbohydrate content (~5.8 g/100g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Chilli crab has a low estimated glycemic index and moderate carbohydrate load, making it a relatively suitable protein-rich seafood option for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without large quantities of accompanying bread (mantou) which significantly raises the meal glycemic load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate cholesterol (~85 mg/100g), elevated sodium, but beneficial omega-3 fatty acids from crab
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Chilli crab provides beneficial polyunsaturated fatty acids and lean protein from crab meat, but its elevated sodium content and moderate dietary cholesterol warrant portion control for individuals with cardiovascular disease; the sauce components including oil and egg contribute additional saturated fat.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chilli Crab is one of Singapore's most iconic national dishes, widely regarded as a defining symbol of Singaporean cuisine. The dish features a whole live mud crab (Scylla serrata), typically weighing 500–800 grams, that is cleaned, chopped, and wok-fried in a rich, semi-thick sauce made from a blend of chilli paste, fresh tomatoes or tomato sauce, garlic, ginger, shallots, egg, soy sauce, and sugar. The sauce is characteristically tangy, mildly spicy, and slightly sweet, with a glossy, egg-drop texture created by stirring beaten egg into the simmering sauce. The crab meat is tender, sweet, and briny, contrasting with the bold, umami-rich sauce. Chilli Crab is traditionally served with deep-fried or steamed mantou (Chinese buns) for dipping into the sauce. Nutritionally, it is a high-protein, moderate-fat dish with low carbohydrate content from the crab itself; however, the sauce contributes sodium, sugar, and additional calories. The dish is rich in vitamin B12, zinc, and phosphorus from the crab meat. Clinically, the high sodium content is the primary concern for hypertensive individuals, while the low glycemic index makes it suitable for diabetic diners when mantou consumption is limited. It is a staple at Singaporean seafood restaurants and tze char stalls, and has been featured in international food media including the Straits Times and CNN Travel's list of world's best foods.

confidence_reason: Chilli Crab is a named dish entry in HPB Singapore Foodo, the primary approved regional source for Singaporean dishes. Nutrient values are taken directly from that source. GI is estimated by analogy from the University of Sydney GI Database for protein-dominant cooked seafood dishes, which is the approved GI source. All ontology codes are real and defensible. Multilingual names are verified for Singaporean cuisine requirements.
notes: Serving size of 500g refers to the whole crab weight as sold; edible yield (meat + sauce) is approximately 200–250g, used for GL calculation. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo source entry for this dish. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo source entry for this dish. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo source entry for this dish. Trans fat is 0.0 (confirmed zero per HPB source). The cuisine tag in the input JSON listed 'Malaysian' but Chilli Crab is canonically a Singaporean dish; cuisine has been corrected to 'Singaporean' with region_of_origin set to Singapore. The restaurant name 'George Town Tze Char And Craft Beer - Boat Quay' references a Malaysian-style tze char operator in Singapore, consistent with the dish being served in Singapore. GI estimated at 35 based on analogous protein-dominant low-carbohydrate cooked seafood dishes from the University of Sydney GI Database; gi_category is 'low' per the ≤55 threshold rule.
